Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria), suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic features. Mildly elevated ALT may indicate hepatic involvement. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological and possibly hepatological evaluation is recommended.
